Invited [Shivani Patel](mailto:spatel@heylo.tech) [ritwik@buildvessel.com](mailto:ritwik@buildvessel.com) [Brian Hart](mailto:bhart@heylo.tech) [Christopher Baron](mailto:cbaron@heylo.tech) [mweaver@heylo.tech](mailto:mweaver@heylo.tech)

Attachments [Prod/Eng Standup](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?eid=N28wNmN1a2F1Y3Y4cWszaW8yYm1vZTRramRfMjAyNjA0MjBUMTUwMDAwWiBzcGF0ZWxAaGV5bG8udGVjaA)

Meeting records [Transcript](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?usp=drive_web&tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh) 

  
  

### Summary

Technology and development processes were discussed for AI assistance and SDLC, with supply chain issues and project management tool adoption also covered.  
  
Technology Integration for SDLC  
The team welcomed a new engineer and discussed integrating AI tools like Claude with systems like GSD and BMAD to improve Software Development Life Cycle practices. The engineer was tasked with driving the implementation of these processes to enhance development efficiency.  
  
Tablet Supply and Project Management  
A shipment of 50 Samsung tablets was largely destroyed, leaving only 10 in stock, necessitating a secure package location for incoming expensive devices. The team is adopting Linear for project management, focusing on better cycle management and prioritization using tags for bugs and customer feedback.  
  
Quarterly Focus and Third-Party Strategy  
The quarterly focus includes a zero issue install feature work, operations improvements, and customer feature expansion to compete with rivals like Grandeare. The team plans to establish a third-party integration pattern by incorporating the Medinder automated medicine dispenser.

  
  

### Next steps

- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Return Medinder: Tell Brian to bring back the Medinder device.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Share Features: Share the prioritized list of new customer features.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Michael Weaver] Develop App: Drive standing up the new standalone Customer Support application.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron, Shivani Patel] Prioritize Features: Review and prioritize the list of Customer Support app features.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Add Placeholder: Create a placeholder for Customer Support app development in the next cycle.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Tie Alerts: Tie device alert emails functionality to site status and live status this week.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron, Ritwik Rastogi] Map Status: Discuss and map out a unified definition for device status and lifecycle. Get back to Shivani.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Configure Demo: Set Kenrest devices to active status but suppress all alert emails this week.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Ritwik Rastogi] Update Form: Update the onboarding form based on recent feedback.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Ritwik Rastogi] Print Codes: Develop and test the QR code printing mechanism in the office tomorrow.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Ritwik Rastogi] Fix Form Link: Investigate and fix forbidden resource error on the onboarding form link by Friday.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Confirm Kits: Check and confirm the current live demo kit count and status with Brian this afternoon.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Send Label: Send a return shipping label to the Howard Center for their kit return.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Update Inventory: Report 2 cameras and update the inventory spreadsheet for Naveen.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Check Tristan: Check with Brian regarding the devices previously sent to Tristan.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Ritwik Rastogi] Check Models: Check the database pinger to find model names for the cameras.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel, Christopher Baron] Schedule Support: Schedule time mid-week to discuss the customer support strategy.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Ritwik Rastogi] Prepare Acha Task: Investigate QR code scanning for tablets and prepare specific tasks for Acha.
    

  
  

### Details

- Introduction and Technology Discussion: Christopher Baron welcomed Michael Weaver to the team, expressing excitement about working together. Michael Weaver mentioned using Notion and asked about Obsidian, a note-taking application that creates a graph of connected notes using markdown files, which can be used with tools like Cursor or Claude for context ([00:00:00](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8vki6w8gcxt2)). They discussed the concept of using Google's GSD (Get Stuff Done), which is a collection of pre-built markdown files that index a codebase to create custom markdown files, enabling quasi-SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) practices ([00:00:54](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8bt1oe2d9b5x)).
    
- AI and Development Processes: Christopher Baron expressed the need for AI-assisted tools like Claude to help engineers write code and integrate relevant context into the repositories ([00:00:00](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8vki6w8gcxt2)). Michael Weaver mentioned a similar system called BMAD used by Chase Bank that follows agile principles and integrates with Cursor for SDLC, updating tools like Jira ([00:00:54](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8bt1oe2d9b5x)). Christopher Baron stated they want Michael Weaver to help drive the implementation of these processes to make their work easier, acknowledging that they are not currently doing enough of the talked-about development process improvements ([00:01:56](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.rluau5ptiy47)).
    
- Discussion of Tablet and Device Supply Issues: Shivani Patel arrived, apologizing for being late due to an ongoing "package fiasco" ([00:01:56](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.rluau5ptiy47)). Shivani Patel reported that a package of tablets had arrived "completely destroyed" and that the required Samsung tablets were now out of stock ([00:02:48](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.wpafapfhw8f)). The team was expecting 50 tablets in total, but only 10 had been received in one shipment ([00:03:48](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.kmi1ynh8ohtz)). Shivani Patel emphasized the need for a secure location for packages and mentioned that expensive tablets are still incoming ([00:02:48](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.wpafapfhw8f)).
    
- Welcome and Team Introductions: Shivani Patel facilitated introductions for Michael Weaver, who had not yet met Ritwik Rastogi. Ritwik Rastogi introduced themself as a computer science graduate who started working with the team five to six months ago in November ([00:03:48](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.kmi1ynh8ohtz)). Michael Weaver introduced themself as a software engineer with over a decade of experience, mentioning their background in Ohio and their interest in technology like Raspberry Pi ([00:04:40](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.ij1db7rqykg7)).
    
- Review of Meeting Structure and Cadence: Shivani Patel explained that the weekly meeting is a new effort to establish structure and standups, primarily to ensure everyone is synchronized on weekly product and customer issues. Since the team is small and frequently meets one-off, these standups are currently the main set cadence, though Shivani Patel anticipates changes as the team evolves ([00:06:37](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.o0tgtwjpqcfg)). Shivani Patel also welcomed feedback on the process, confirming they are in "trial and error mode" ([00:07:31](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.jcph7uegth4b)).
    
- Project Management Tooling (Linear): Shivani Patel introduced Linear, the chosen project management tool, which was recommended by Christopher Baron for its ease of use in creating, assigning, and adding flows, calling cycles "sprints". The team is focusing on improving how cycles are managed, moving away from a "laundry list" of items to better prioritize work and use tags for bugs and customer feedback ([00:07:31](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.jcph7uegth4b)). Shivani Patel noted that better structure, including processes for customer support and ticketing, will be critical before the expected "significantly more installs happening through the summer" ([00:08:45](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.9nn4pduwl198)).
    
- Quarterly Focus Areas: Shivani Patel outlined the main focus areas for the current quarter: "zero issue install" (feature work to ensure smooth installs), operations work (intake, inventory, customer support), and "customer feature expansion" ([00:08:45](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.9nn4pduwl198)). The latter includes new features to compete with competitors and motivate customers to use Halo ([00:09:47](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.yhwl7qfgdx66)).
    
- Medinder Integration and Third-Party Strategy: The team discussed the planned integration with Medinder, a third-party automated medicine dispenser, to address medication and fall management, which are two major issues for residents ([00:09:47](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.yhwl7qfgdx66)). Christopher Baron will lead this integration, which is significant because it establishes the pattern for future third-party integrations, such as for health devices ([00:11:00](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.735rrwyrilju)).
    
- Alert Escalation and Resident Tasks: Ritwik Rastogi asked for clarification on "escalation of alerts," which Shivani Patel explained as protocols for unresolved critical alerts. The current system displays critical issues on the dashboard but lacks automatic triggers, but the team plans to implement generic escalations, such as pinging an admin if a critical alert remains unresolved for a couple of hours ([00:11:51](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.plkrvybr122d)). Shivani Patel also confirmed that resident tasks (a checklist for residents), which are offered by their competitor Grandeare, are being prioritized for development this quarter ([00:12:49](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.yeupbe11obax)).
    
- Review of Last Week's Work and Current Commitments: Christopher Baron summarized that Ritwik Rastogi focused on inventory and provisioning, while Christopher Baron focused on enabling and deploying audio from the cameras ([00:15:00](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8xth7dswbqfm)). For the current week, Christopher Baron is finalizing code for customer support, provisioning, and shiftability roles, moving on to the agency demo environment, and shipping code reviews from Acha. Christopher Baron is also working with Michael Weaver on case load management and ensuring Ritwik Rastogi is unblocked on inventory onboarding ([00:15:58](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.r6lyihlme8c)).
    
- Customer Support App Development: Christopher Baron suggested creating a new, standalone customer support application, possibly led by Michael Weaver, in the next sprint, budgeting three to four weeks for development ([00:16:42](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.e9wsrq18p9yt)). They plan to discuss prioritizing the list of required customer support features ([00:17:36](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.9cyf91pap5do)). Shivani Patel agreed to put a placeholder for this project in the next cycle to deprecate the current customer support experience ([00:18:19](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.v7xgmyv2hpki)).
    
- Site Status, Demo State, and Device Alerts: Christopher Baron intends to tie device alerts to the site status this week, requiring a site to be "live" before sending device alert emails ([00:18:19](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.v7xgmyv2hpki)). The team discussed Kenrest, a multi-state agency doing a simulated test using a lightweight kit, and the need to ensure the site is in a "demo" state where emails are suppressed but the devices do not appear inactive on the dashboard ([00:19:25](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.66p4buv07sg6)) ([00:25:52](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.49bk8p9wlluz)). Christopher Baron confirmed they can update the email checkers to prevent emails if the site is in a demo state, which fits the Kenrest use case perfectly ([00:27:14](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.750hmwpj51j)) ([00:28:41](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.u80d4r866yta)).
    
- Device Life Cycle and Inventory Alignment: Shivani Patel raised concerns about the different naming conventions for device status in the platform ("life cycle") versus in inventory ([00:21:10](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.ljam2pthbmis)). Ritwik Rastogi suggested that inventory should manage states before provisioning (e.g., in warehouse, damaged), while the platform manages post-provisioning ([00:21:58](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.f1rk3cna0z5e)). They all agreed that post-provisioning device status/life cycle must align between the platform and inventory to avoid confusion and properly track subscription costs after the first device ping ([00:22:55](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.59pb9pib9oze)). Christopher Baron committed to working with Ritwik Rastogi to map out a single status definition ([00:25:01](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.93zvx0x5n70k)).
    
- Case Load Management View: Christopher Baron presented a revised view for case load management, proposing a calendar-like view that color-codes people and sites instead of the existing vertical hours view. Shivani Patel confirmed that customers prefer a calendar view with week and month options, similar to Outlook, and that the view should allow toggling between organizing by site and by person ([00:29:32](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.dt0qvos715k3)).
    
- Inventory Updates and Onboarding Form: Ritwik Rastogi provided an update on inventory work, including adding functionality for uploading receipts, editing line items, and configuring hardware components. Ritwik Rastogi also reported that the onboarding form needs updating based on feedback and is targeted for completion by Monday for Shivani Patel to use on site with customers next week ([00:31:20](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.rgl0zadmsjux)). Ritwik Rastogi plans to work on the QR printer integration on Tuesday, coinciding with a planned office visit for live testing ([00:32:31](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.9aud2jrvwwxm)).
    
- QR Code Generation Plan: The team confirmed their plan for QR code generation, which involves using a six-digit alphanumeric code to provide approximately 60 million possibilities ([00:34:32](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.aqs8x5bfloem)). This six-digit choice was made to prevent the code from becoming overly complicated and susceptible to read errors from the printer ([00:36:21](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.yvz09bjfyw8m)). The V1 process will involve an independent Python script generating the codes, printing them via a ZPL layout, and then scanning them into the inventory system, where the app will catch and reject any rare duplicates ([00:35:12](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.c9mk3kr1i138)).
    
- Future Provisioning and Customer Install Load: Shivani Patel committed to following up on the status of devices in the office, including checking with Brian on the live count of demo kits and devices sent to Tristan ([00:40:03](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.twn8i33qyoeh)). Shivani Patel noted that upcoming customer installs, including MCDs, Big Lakes, JCDS, and Penrest, could potentially happen simultaneously with the New York installs, requiring the team to brace for a heavy load ([00:38:54](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.oicm6f44pijf)). The team discussed the need for a stable provisioning solution beyond just interns ([00:47:45](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.vtrh20q0xpun)).
    
- Work for Intern (Acha) and Bot Development: Christopher Baron suggested Acha could continue working on the QR code scanning provisioning for tablets, which they had previously hit a wall on ([00:44:17](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.1rhng9pewqhi)). Ritwik Rastogi agreed to investigate the technical issues and provide Acha with a path forward ([00:45:08](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.15be0ucj95pr)). Christopher Baron also suggested the "moonshotty" idea of having Acha explore writing a bot to automate Real Link app management, especially since Acha will be working remotely ([00:45:57](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RTXCBj_0bTf5FrJfPszaAnsD0AViWswCAQ8zXBC67GE/edit?tab=t.pkz7moil6ngh#heading=h.8rnvoe45scwe)).
    

  
  

You should review Gemini's notes to make sure they're accurate. [Get tips and learn how Gemini takes notes](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/14754931)

How is the quality of these specific notes? [Take a short survey](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_9vK3UZEaIQKKE7A?confid=FzvpOO4nnAuePaoxI0J7DxIPOAIIigIgABgBCA&detailid=standard&screenshot=false) to let us know your feedback, including how helpful the notes were for your needs.
